ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather data */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was collected */,
  "min_temp" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded */,
  "max_temp" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded */,
  "rainfall" REAL /* Rainfall recorded */,
  "evaporation" REAL /* Evaporation recorded */,
  "sunshine" REAL /* Sunshine duration recorded */,
  "wind_gust_dir" TEXT /* Direction of the wind gust */,
  "wind_gust_speed" INTEGER /* Speed of the wind gust */,
  "wind_dir_9am" TEXT /* Wind direction at 9 AM */,
  "wind_dir_3pm" TEXT /* Wind direction at 3 PM */,
  "wind_speed_9am" INTEGER /* Wind speed at 9 AM */,
  "wind_speed_3pm" INTEGER /* Wind speed at 3 PM */,
  "humidity_9am" INTEGER /* Humidity at 9 AM */,
  "humidity_3pm" INTEGER /* Humidity at 3 PM */,
  "pressure_9am" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure at 9 AM */,
  "pressure_3pm" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure at 3 PM */,
  "cloud_9am" INTEGER /* Cloud cover at 9 AM */,
  "cloud_3pm" INTEGER /* Cloud cover at 3 PM */,
  "temp_9am" REAL /* Temperature at 9 AM */,
  "temp_3pm" REAL /* Temperature at 3 PM */,
  "rain_today" TEXT /* Whether it rained today (Yes/No) */,
  "rain_tomorrow" TEXT /* Whether it will rain tomorrow (Yes/No) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the data entry */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., reliable, suspect) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., manual entry, sensor reading) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the location is situated */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the location */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the location */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the station is situated */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "sensor_types" TEXT /* Types of sensors installed at the station */,
  "station_status" TEXT /* Current status of the station (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number for station contact */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email for station contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_stations_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the sensor type */,
  "range" TEXT /* Range of the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* Active status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station being maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance activity */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., calibration, repair) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the maintenance activity */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the maintenance activity (in minutes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data used for the forecast */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_type" TEXT /* Type of forecast (e.g., rain, temperature) */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of the forecasted event */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details or notes about the forecast */,
  "model_used" TEXT /* Name of the model used for the forecast */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("weather_id")
)
